Japanese: ９月１４日　午前　９時４１分 地方裁判所　被告人第４控え室
English: October 14, 9:41AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 4

Japanese: なっ、なるほどくん！
English: Hey! Nick!

Japanese: どうした？ また、何かあったの‥‥？
English: What is it this time?

Japanese: ものスゴい人気だよ、 今日の法廷も！
English: You won't believe how many people are here for the trial!

Japanese: まあ‥‥殺人事件だからなあ。
English: Well... It is a murder case.

Japanese: ナニ言ってんの。 おとなりの法廷だよ！
English: What are you talking about? They're here for the trial next door!

Japanese: “おとなり”‥‥？
English: "Next door"...?

Japanese: ホラ！　アイガ探偵の 裁判があるんだよ！　今日。
English: Why don't you know this, Nick? *sigh* They're having Detective Atmey's trial today!

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙探偵の‥‥？
English: Detective Atmey...?

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクとして 裁判にかけられるんだって。
English: They say they're going to try him as Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: も、もう？　早いな‥‥
English: Already? That was fast...

Japanese: あたしも見たかったなあ、 仮面マスクの裁判。
English: Boy, I'd love to see Mask☆DeMasque's trial.

Japanese: ‥‥どうせ‥‥
English: ...I know...

Japanese: そういえば、春美ちゃんは？
English: By the way, where's Pearls?

Japanese: ああ。里に帰ったよ。 これ以上、修行を休めない、って。
English: Oh, she went back home. She said she can't neglect her training anymore.

Japanese: ‥‥どうせ、ボクなんか‥‥
English: ...I know you don't like me...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥最近、 さらに熱心になったね。修行。
English: Pearls has really gotten into her training lately, huh?

Japanese: うん。 ‥‥１年前の事件から、かな。
English: Yeah... Ever since... ...that incident last year.

Japanese: ボクのコト、 ムシしないでくださーーーい！
English: Pleeeeease! Don't ignore meeee!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、優作さん。 おはようございます！
English: ...Oh, Mr. DeLite! Good morning!

Japanese: どうせ、ボクなんて‥‥
English: No one likes me...

Japanese: サツジンを犯しても、ダレにも 注目されないんですよね‥‥
English: No one would notice me, even if I killed someone...

Japanese: いやいや、そんなコトないですよ。 ‥‥って、ゆ、優作さん！
English: Come on, don't be silly! W-Wait a sec!

Japanese: ま、ま、まさか‥‥ きみが、はは、ハンニン‥‥！
English: You don't mean... You're the murderer...!?

Japanese: あやややや、ちちちちがいますッ！ ボク、しがない怪人でーーーす！
English: N-N-N-No, no! I'm just a poor thief!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥いや。ヘンか。 怪人に“しがない”も“ナイス”も
English: No, wait, that's not right. A thief can't really be "poor"

Japanese: （天杉　優作の主張によると‥‥）
English: (Now, let's see... According to Mr. DeLite...)

Japanese: （怪人の２回目からの犯行は、 計画書にしたがっただけ‥‥）
English: (From his second crime on, he was following a bunch of set plans.)

Japanese: （だれかが彼に犯行を指示して、 エモノを取りあげていたらしい）
English: (Plans that someone had been sending to him to "help" him commit the heists.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。怪人のコト‥‥ サツジンに関係あるの？
English: Do you think there's a connection between the thief and the murder, Nick?

Japanese: たぶんね。‥‥とにかく、 今日の法廷はスピード勝負だよ。
English: It's possible... But today's trial is a race against the clock.

Japanese: え、どうして？ いつもどおり、のんびり行こうよ。
English: Huh? How come? Let's just take our time like always.

Japanese: それじゃあ‥‥ ちょっと、マズいんだよ。
English: I'm afraid... That's not an option.

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第６法廷
English: October 14, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 6

Japanese: これより、天杉　優作の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Ron DeLite.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ジュンビ‥‥か。 オクビョウ者の逃げ口上だぜ。
English: You're "ready"? Preparation is the last refuge of the weak.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。そういうワケで、 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: ...OK, settle down everyone. Let's begin with your opening statement, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥すっかり 慣れちまったみたいだな）
English: (Ugh... He's got the judge in the palm of his hands... Yet again...)

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、戦場に出るには 若すぎた。‥‥それだけだぜ。
English: Ron DeLite is simply too young to be sent to war. ...That's all.

Japanese: さすがに、それだけでは ゼンゼンわかりません。
English: I'm afraid I have no idea what that means, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 世話のやける連中だぜ。
English: Ha...! Then you need to get out more, Your Honor.

Japanese: 人生は戦場だ。だが‥‥だからこそ スジは通さなきゃいけねえ。
English: Life is war. But that is exactly why you must be more precise in your wording.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトだ。 わかるだろ？
English: That's all my statement means. You understand now, right?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。では、事件のあらましを カクニンしておきましょう。
English: ...Yes. Well then, let me briefly summarize the details of this case.

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥ ヤリ手の司会者みたいだな）
English: (Wow... The judge is taking charge like he knows what's going on for a change...)

Japanese: 被害者は毒島　黒兵衛。 ＫＢ警備会社の社長でした。
English: The victim is Kane Bullard, CEO of KB Security.

Japanese: 彼の死体が金庫の中から発見された のは、１３日の朝、９時ごろ。
English: His body was found in a safe at approximately 9 AM on the morning of the 13th.

Japanese: しかし‥‥殺害されたのは、 前日の午前１時だった。
English: However, the time of death was estimated as 1 AM of the previous day.

Japanese: そして‥‥その時刻。現場で、 迷子のコネコちゃんが鳴いていた。
English: And... that's when our little lost kitten dropped the ball.

Japanese: ソイツが‥‥ そこの被告人、ってワケだぜ。
English: That little lost kitten is, of course, the defendant.

Japanese: それでは、ゴドー検事。 最初の証人を呼んでください。
English: Very well then, Mr. Godot. Please call your first witness.

Japanese: ‥‥オレは、裁判中のコーヒーは １７杯まで、と決めている。
English: I never drink more than 17 cups of coffee during any given trial.

Japanese: だが。ホンモノはいつだって‥‥ 最初の１杯目だけ、だぜ。
English: But the first one... is always the best.

Japanese: あの。最初の証人を‥‥
English: Um, Mr. Godot... Your witness...?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、被告人・天杉　優作の 証言を聞くとするぜ。
English: ...OK, then. Let's hear what the defendant, Mr. Ron DeLite, has to say for himself.

Japanese: ひ、被告人‥‥？ いかがですか、弁護人。
English: The defendant...? Well, Mr. Wright? Does the defense have any objections?

Japanese: 被告人が証人となると、 弁護側には不利になりますが‥‥
English: It may be a bit of a disadvantage having the defendant testify, but...

Japanese: （ムカシ、千尋さんが ぼくを弁護してくれたとき‥‥）
English: (I remember when Mia was defending me...)

Japanese: （彼女は、被告人である ぼくの尋問を受け入れた‥‥）
English: (She allowed me to testify so she could do the cross-examination...)

Japanese: （このぼくを‥‥ “信頼”してくれていたから）
English: (She put a lot of trust in me back then...)

Japanese: 弁護側に異議はありません。 被告人を証人として認めます。
English: We have no objections, Your Honor. The defense will allow Mr. DeLite to testify.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ いいカクゴだ、まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! You've got guts, Trite.

Japanese: それでは、 天杉　優作を証言台へ！
English: Alright then, Mr. Ron DeLite, please take the stand!

Japanese: ‥‥やったんだろ？　アンタが。
English: ...You did it. Didn't you?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: あ。‥‥いえいえいえいえいえいえ めめ。めっそうもありません！
English: Uh... No no no no no no no! Th-Th-Th-That's not true!

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。
English: ...Hmm.

Japanese: 裁判の最短キロクが 生まれるかと思いました。
English: For a moment there I thought we'd set the record for the shortest trial ever.

Japanese: 優作さん、見た目だけですでに、 半分有罪だもんね‥‥。
English: *sigh* Well, Mr. DeLite already looks plenty guilty with that face he's making...

Japanese: クチを開けば、もう半分も有罪 って感じなんだよな‥‥。
English: And once he opens his big mouth he'll probably put the last nail in his own coffin...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　いいだろうさ。
English: Ha...! Very well.

Japanese: なあ、アンタ‥‥。 聞かせてくれねえか？
English: Now then... Can you tell me something?

Japanese: 社長を殺していないなら‥‥ なぜ、ＫＢ警備へ行ったんだ？
English: If you didn't kill Bullard, why did you go to KB Security?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥その。 言いにくいんだよなあ。帰りたいな
English: W-W-Well, I... That's kind of hard to say. Boy, I wish I could go home

Japanese: では、そのあたりのことを 証言してもらいましょう！
English: Now then, let's hear some testimony about what happened!

Japanese: ～ＫＢ警備に行った理由～
English: -- My Visit To KB Security --

Japanese: あの晩、夜中の１時ごろ、 ＫＢ警備の社長室をたずねました。
English: That evening, around 1 AM, I went to see Mr. Bullard in his office at KB Security.

Japanese: 呼び出しのお手紙‥‥その。 脅迫状をもらったものですから。
English: The blackmail letter I got... It ordered me to go there.

Japanese: １年前まで社員でしたから、 社長室の場所は知ってました。
English: I'd been working for KB Security until a year ago, so I knew where his office was.

Japanese: 夜中の１時‥‥というと、 まさに犯行時間のころですな。
English: 1 AM... The exact time the murder took place.

Japanese: ‥‥弱者は運命に流され、 強者はそれを飲みほす。
English: The weak get washed away by the tides of fate... The strong drink it up.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　今日もニガいぜ。 ‥‥オレの運命。
English: Ha...! It's bitter today, too... Just like my destiny.

Japanese: なんだかんだ言って コーヒー飲んでるよ。
English: You'd never know that from the way he's chugging it down.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。‥‥尋問を。
English: Mr. Wright... Your cross-examination, if you please.

Japanese: ～ＫＢ警備に行った理由～
English: -- My Visit To KB Security --

Japanese: あの晩、夜中の１時ごろ、 ＫＢ警備の社長室をたずねました。
English: That evening, around 1 AM, I went to see Mr. Bullard in his office at KB Security.

Japanese: 呼び出しのお手紙‥‥その。 脅迫状をもらったものですから。
English: The blackmail letter I got... It ordered me to go there.

Japanese: １年前まで社員でしたから、 社長室の場所は知ってました。
English: I'd been working for KB Security until a year ago, so I knew where his office was.

Japanese: なんか‥‥ミョーに少ないよね。 優作さんの証言。
English: Hmm... There wasn't much to his testimony, was there?

Japanese: きっと、何か言いたくない コトでもあるんじゃないかな。
English: Sounds like he's avoiding something... At least, that's what it sounds like to me.

Japanese: え。なんか、イヤな予感‥‥。 そっとしておこうか。
English: Uh oh. I've got a bad feeling about this... We'd better be careful.

Japanese: でも、それを聞き出さないと たどりつけないからね。
English: But if we don't find a way to make him spill the beans...

Japanese: ‥‥真相に。
English: ...we'll never get closer to the truth.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Yeah...

Japanese: 社長の脅迫はコワくなかったです。 バラされて困ることもなかったし。
English: The blackmail thread didn't scare me. It wasn't going to cause me trouble or anything.

Japanese: 会社の機密情報を売って、クビに。 まれかちゃんにはヒミツですけど。
English: He fired me for selling company secrets... but Dessie doesn't know about that.

Japanese: 午前１時‥‥というのは、 まちがいないんですか？
English: 1 AM, huh... You're absolutely sure about that?

Japanese: ええ。社長室に入るときに 時計を見ましたから‥‥‥‥いや。
English: Yes. That's what my watch said when I was entering the CEO's office... Err, no...

Japanese: 待てよ。それって、どうなんだろ。 時計がおくれて、ぼくのハラ時計も
English: Actually, I'm not really sure. My watch was slow, and my internal clock was also a bit

Japanese: “午前１時”といえば、被害者の 毒島氏が殺害された時間ですな。
English: 1 AM... That's the exact time the victim, Mr. Bullard was murdered, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥いっしょにコーヒーを飲むには おそすぎる時間、だぜ。
English: It's too late for a coffee date, that's for sure.

Japanese: “呼び出しのお手紙”ですか‥‥
English: It "ordered you there"?

Japanese: どこかに呼び出される 脅迫状は、初めてでしたね。
English: It was the first time I'd gotten a blackmail letter that ordered me to go somewhere.

Japanese: そうでない脅迫状は、 初めてではなかったのですか？
English: Does that mean you've gotten other blackmail letters then?

Japanese: ええ、それはもう。 あれを盗めとか、これを奪えとか、
English: Oh, of course. They'd say things like, "Steal this" or, "Take that".

Japanese: いやいや！　その話は、また今度！ （よけいなコト、言うなよ‥‥）
English: Aha ha! Why don't you save those for later, Mr. DeLite? (Pleeease don't say any more!)

Japanese: （‥‥さて、どうしよう？）
English: (...Now, what should do I do?)

Japanese: その脅迫状には、 どんなコトが‥‥？
English: So what did the blackmail letter in question say...?

Japanese: ５００万円を持ってこい、って。
English: It said to bring $50,000.

Japanese: ‥‥カネ、か。サツジンの 動機として、じゅうぶんだぜ。
English: Money, eh. A perfect motive for committing murder.

Japanese: あ！　でも！でもでも！ ちょっと待ってくださーーーい！
English: Oh! But! W-Wait! Waaaaaaaaait!

Japanese: ボク、おカネを払うつもり なんて、ありませんでしたよ！
English: I never intended to pay that money anyway!

Japanese: ほお‥‥ そうなんですか？
English: Oh...? Is that right?

Japanese: だって！　ボクには、脅迫されて 困ることなんて、ありませんから！
English: After all, he had nothing to hold over my head! I had nothing to be afraid of!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それは重要な ポイントですな‥‥
English: Hmm... An important point indeed.

Japanese: 証人。今の発言を 証言に加えていただきましょう。
English: Witness, let's have that added to your testimony.

Japanese: はい！
English: Yes, sir!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
（だいじょうぶか‥‥？ 優作くん‥‥）
English: (A muddy mudskipper in outer space has a better chance of surviving than I do.)

Japanese: （優作くんの場合‥‥ヘタに ゆさぶると、イノチ取りになる）
English: (I can't press Ron too hard... It could ruin the case.)

Japanese: （ここはひとつ、 オンビンに行こう‥‥）
English: (I'll just take this one slowly...)

Japanese: なんか、政治家みたいだね、 なるほどくん。
English: Wow Nick, you kind of look like a politician, planning out your next move...

Japanese: たしか‥‥ＫＢ警備会社の 警備隊長だったんですよね？
English: You used to be a security chief for KB Security, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ、まあ。
English: ...Yes, that's right.

Japanese: たた、たいちょう？ ああ、あなたが？
English: A security chief? You?

Japanese: それが‥‥今から１年前。 突然、クビになった‥‥
English: And yet... A year ago, you were fired without notice...

Japanese: クビにされた恨み、か。 動機として、じゅうぶんだぜ。
English: Revenge for an old grudge? A perfect motive for murder, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （‥‥マズいな。話題を 変えたほうがいいか‥‥？）
English: (This isn't good. Maybe I should change the subject...?)

Japanese: ＫＢ警備を、クビになった あとは、何を‥‥？
English: What did you do after being fired from KB Security?

Japanese: ボク、いろいろシゴトを 探したんですけど‥‥
English: I spent a lot of time looking for work...

Japanese: やっぱり、自分で何かを 始めようと思ったんです。
English: But then I decided I wanted to start something myself.

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ マズい質問だったか‥‥！）
English: (Not good! This is going from bad to worse...)

Japanese: それで、本やテレビ、映画を いっぱい見て、怪人の勉強をして。
English: So I started looking at books, TV, and movies, learning all I could about masked men.

Japanese: か、“カイジン”‥‥？
English: M-Masked men...?

Japanese: ま、マンガを描こうと思った ワケですね？　‥‥カイジンの！
English: S-So you wanted to try drawing a comic, right? ...About masked men!

Japanese: いえいえ。そうじゃなくて、 ぼくがカイジンにですね‥‥
English: No no, that's not right. I wanted to become--

Japanese: 裁判長！　とにかく、 先に進みましょう！
English: Your Honor! Let's move along, shall we!?

Japanese: ‥‥自分で質問したくせに、 おかしなヒトですね‥‥
English: But you asked the question... You're being quite odd today, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥また 話がこじれるところだった）
English: (Way to bring the spinning buzz saw one notch closer to your own neck, Ace Attorney.)

Japanese: 優作くん。クビになった理由を 聞かせてください。
English: Mr. DeLite. Please tell us why you were fired from your job.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: W-Well...

Japanese: 『聞かなきゃよかった』‥‥ 世の中、そんなのばっかりだぜ。
English: The world is filled with those who have said, "I wish I had never asked that."

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあやっぱり‥‥
English: O-OK, then I take it ba--

Japanese: ‥‥被告人。 証言してください。
English: ...Defendant. Please answer the question.

Japanese: あの‥‥ボク。 おカネが必要だったんです。
English: I... Well. I needed money.

Japanese: おカネ‥‥？
English: You needed money...?

Japanese: その‥‥まれかちゃんが おカネつかうの、シュミだから‥‥
English: Um, well, you see... Dessie loves to spend it. It's kind of her hobby...

Japanese: イヤなシュミだねー、 なるほどくん。
English: Not exactly the best hobby in the world, huh, Nick?

Japanese: 給料じゃ、とても足りなくて‥‥
English: My salary wasn't nearly enough...

Japanese: ボク、会社からデータを 盗んじゃったんです。
English: So, I stole data from the company.
...Come again?

Japanese: ＫＢ警備って、いろんな会社の セキュリティの情報が集まるし‥‥
English: KB Security has a lot of security info on all sorts of companies...

Japanese: ボク、いちおう警備チームの 隊長だったから‥‥
English: And since I was a security team chief...

Japanese: データを盗んで‥‥ 売っちまったワケだ。
English: You stole some data... and sold it.

Japanese: それが、社長にバレちゃって。 ‥‥懲戒免職に。
English: Mr. Bullard found out, and I was fired immediately.

Japanese: ええええええええッ！ （聞かなきゃよかった‥‥）
English: Whaaaaaaaaaa! (I wish I had never asked that...)

Japanese: なんとか、内密にしてもらって。 自分からやめたコトにしたんです。
English: I was somehow able to keep it secret, and made it seem like I had quit on my own.

Japanese: なるほどくん、なあに？ チョーカイメンショクって。
English: What is it, Nick? You don't look so good.

Japanese: 会社に損害を与えた人間を、 バツとしてクビにするコト、だぜ。
English: Someone who brings harm to their company is fired as punishment.

Japanese: アンタは特に、よく覚えて おいたほうがよさそうだな。
English: You'd do well to remember that.

Japanese: ‥‥言われちゃったよ。
English: ...He sure told you.

Japanese: 証人。会社のデータを盗んだ ‥‥ジジツなのですね？
English: So you admit that you stole data from your company, is that correct?

Japanese: は、はい。 ご、ごめんなさい‥‥
English: Y-Yes. I'm sorry...

Japanese: このジジツは重要と考えます。 ‥‥証言に加えてもらいましょう！
English: This is a very important fact. Please add it to your testimony!

Japanese: （これで‥‥状況はガラリと 変わってしまったな‥‥）
English: (Oh man... This whole thing just took a big turn for the worse, crashed, and blew up.)

Japanese: （‥‥よくない方向に）
English: (...It's gonna take the jaws of life to rip this case from the clutches of disaster.)

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、この話題は やりすごしたほうがいいな）
English: (I should just avoid this whole subject... At least for now.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 弁護側もダマりこんだようですし、 やはり、動機と考えられます。
English: As the defense appears to have nothing to say, it seems that this is indeed your motive.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥ボク！ 社長を恨んでなんか‥‥！
English: W-What? That's not true! I didn't have any hard feelings towards Mr. Bullard!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥！ 何か言っておくべきだったか）
English: (Ack! I knew I should've said something!)

Japanese: そもそも、何を“脅迫” されていたんですか？
English: Just what were you being "blackmailed" about anyway?

Japanese: 脅迫状には《正体をばらされたく なければ》と書いてありますね。
English: The blackmail letter said, "If you don't want your identity revealed," correct?

Japanese: それはモチロン、怪人☆仮面マスク のコトだったんでしょうね。
English: I'm sure it was referring to the whole Mask☆DeMasque thing.

Japanese: でもボク、社長にはシッポを つかまれていませんでしたから。
English: But I wasn't worried. Mr. Bullard didn't have anything on me.

Japanese: “社長には”‥‥？
English: He didn't...?

Japanese: とにかく、ボク。世間からナニを 言われても、かまわないんです。
English: Anyway, I don't care what anyone says about me.

Japanese: まれかちゃんさえ、ボクを 信じてくれれば。
English: Just as long as Dessie believes in me.

Japanese: （そのまれかさんは、優作くんが 怪人だと信じてなかったワケか）
English: (So that's why Ms. DeLite didn't believe he was Mask☆DeMasque.)

Japanese: だから、ボクを脅したって、 ムダだったんですよ！
English: That's why I knew they were just hollow threats!
Hmm...

Japanese: そもそも、何を“脅迫” されていたんですか？
English: Just what were you being "blackmailed" about anyway?

Japanese: 脅迫状には《正体をばらされたく なければ》と書いてありますね。
English: The blackmail letter said, "If you don't want your identity revealed," correct?

Japanese: それはモチロン、怪人☆仮面マスク のコトだったんでしょうね。
English: I'm sure it was referring to the whole Mask☆DeMasque thing.

Japanese: おっと‥‥今となっちゃ、 それは通らないぜ‥‥。
English: Tsk, tsk. I'm afraid that reason isn't going to hold up anymore.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 被害者は、被告人が怪人だと証明 する証拠は持っていなかった。
English: The victim had no proof the defendant was Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: だが‥‥アンタ、１年前に 会社の情報を盗んだ犯人なんだぜ。
English: But... You were the one who stole company secrets only one year earlier.

Japanese: あ‥‥で、でも！　その件では、 クビになって決着がつきました！
English: B-B-But... That was all over with once I was fired!

Japanese: たしかに、情報を盗んだことは 内密に処理してもらったケド‥‥
English: But it is true that the fact I stole secrets was kept under wraps...

Japanese: い、今さら‥‥おカネを払う いわれなんて、ありません！
English: Even so, there was no reason for me to pay up now, a whole year later!

Japanese: （ホントに‥‥そうだろうか？）
English: (Hmm... Is that really true?)

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What is it, Nick?

Japanese: 《会社のデータを盗んだ》という ジジツ‥‥
English: The fact that Ron was stealing company data...

Japanese: 優作くん、それを公表されて、 困ることはなかったのかな‥‥？
English: I wonder if he would've had a problem if that ever became public knowledge...?

Japanese: ‥‥いや。なかったな。
English: ...Nah, I guess not.

Japanese: “あるかも”って言い出したの、 なるほどくんじゃない！
English: But didn't you just say he might!?

Japanese: イヤなコトからは目をそらす‥‥ それが、ぼくのやり方だぜ。
English: Always avoid unpleasant or inconvenient facts... That's one of my rules.

Japanese: カッコつけて逃げちゃダメでしょ！
English: Don't try to get out of this by acting all cool!

Japanese: ‥‥優作くん。 きみは、とても困ったはずだよ。
English: Mr. DeLite... I believe you would have been in considerable trouble...

Japanese: 《会社の情報を盗んだ》という きみの正体がバラされたら‥‥。
English: ...if your identity as a company data thief was made public.
Urk!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい、 成歩堂くん！
English: M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 弁護士のあなたが、被告人を いじめてどうするのです！
English: What are you trying to do by bullying your own client!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
オトコはヒドい目にあって 成長するもの、だぜ。
English: Men are like colonies of bacteria. The more heat you apply, the faster they grow.

Japanese: そ、そういうものです！
English: Th-That's exactly right!

Japanese: じゃ、ヒドい目にあってもらうか。 なあ、弁護士クン！
English: Well then, feel free to keep up the heat, my little lawyer amigo!

Japanese: え！　ぼ、ぼくがですか？
English: L-Little lawyer amigo...?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタの主張‥‥ キッチリと立証してもらうぜ。
English: Yes, now come on. Provide some evidence to back up your assertion.

Japanese: 天杉が《会社の情報を盗んだ》コト を公表されたくなかった理由とは！
English: Why would Mr. DeLite want to keep his data stealing secret from going public!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ どうだい、被告人さんよォ。
English: Ha...! Well, Mr. Defendant?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥だそうです。
English: ...You heard the defendant.

Japanese: （ハラ立つな‥‥）
English: (Just when I thought it wasn't possible, a whole new level of irritation rises before me...)

Japanese: どうやら、弁護人の ひとり遊びだったようですな。
English: It looks like the defense was just playing with himself.

Japanese: （せめて“ひとりズモウ”ぐらい 言ってほしかった‥‥）
English: (Do you think you could rephrase that, Your Honor...? For my sake...?)

Japanese: では！　証言に 戻っていただきましょう。
English: Well, let's get back to the testimony, shall we?

Japanese: どうして、そんなことを‥‥？
English: Why would you do something like that?

Japanese: まれかちゃんのシュミの ためかな。やっぱり。
English: Well, for Dessie's hobby. What else?

Japanese: （ムダづかい、か‥‥）
English: Wasting money, huh...?

Japanese: ムダなんかじゃ、 ありませーーーん！
English: It's not a waaaaaste!

Japanese: まれかさん、知らないんだよね。 優作さんがクビになったこと。
English: So Ms. DeLite doesn't know that her husband was fired, does she?

Japanese: そうみたいだな。
English: So it would seem.

Japanese: ビミョーだよねえ。 ヒミツのある夫婦、って。
English: I'm not sure what to think about couples who keep secrets like that from each other.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんも、 夫婦を語るようになったか‥‥）
English: (I can't believe it. This case has gotten even Maya to think seriously about couples...)

Japanese: 弁護人。遠い目をしないように。
English: Please try to stay focused, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 優作くん。‥‥きのう聞いた話と、 ちょっとちがいますね。
English: Mr. DeLite... What you said just now doesn't match what you told me yesterday.

Japanese: え、え‥‥ナニがですか？
English: Huh...? What doesn't?

Japanese: きみは‥‥ 何よりも恐れていたはずだ。
English: I think you must have been scared... Very scared.

Japanese: きみのヒミツを、ある人物に 知られてしまうことを‥‥
English: Of having a certain person find out your secret...

Japanese: はうぐ。
English: *gulp*

Japanese: ある人物‥‥？
English: A certain person...?

Japanese: ‥‥天杉　希華さん。 被告人の奥さんです。
English: Ms. Desirée DeLite. The defendant's wife.

Japanese: うあ！　‥‥ぼ、ぼくッ！
English: Waah! But I-I...!

Japanese: あの。なんて言うか、 まれかちゃんはその。
English: L-Listen to me! My Dessie! She's...!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、ウカウカしてると シゴトを取られちまいそうだぜ。
English: ...Looks like if I just sit back and relax, the fun will end before it truly begins.

Japanese: （ゴドー検事‥‥！）
English: (Grr! Godot!)

Japanese: そう。すべては‥‥ そのカミさんが原因だったのさ。
English: Yes, we know. It was all your wife's fault.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: カミさんの小づかいのため、 会社の機密データを盗んだ。
English: Mr. DeLite stole company data to pay for his wife's spending habit, for which he was fired.

Japanese: クビになったことをカミさんに 知られたくなくて、脅迫された。
English: Unable to face his own wife, someone used his dirty little secret to blackmail him.

Japanese: そして、殺人事件が起こったのさ！
English: And that is how this murder came about!
Oh... Hmm...!

Japanese: （くそ！　また、 アイツの思うツボか‥‥）
English: (No! Everything is falling neatly into place for him!)

Japanese: ま、まれかちゃんのコト。 そんなふうに言うと‥‥
English: D-Don't talk about my Dessie like that...

Japanese: ユルしませえええーーーーん！
English: Or you'll be sooooooorry!!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: Well, it seems...

Japanese: いろいろなコトが ハッキリしてきました。
English: ...that we've learned a great deal of things here, so far.

Japanese: どう？　なるほどくん。
English: What do you think, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥開廷から２０分で、ここまで 不利になるとは思わなかったよ。
English: I didn't think it was possible to get so thoroughly whipped in just 20 minutes...

Japanese: 動機はじゅうぶんです。
English: Clearly there was sufficient motive for murder.

Japanese: 奥さんのためにデータを盗み、 ヒミツを守るために、殺人を‥‥。
English: He stole data for his wife, and he killed to protect his secret...

Japanese: 行きすぎた家族サービス、 とでも言いましょうか。
English: A family man who cared just a little too much.

Japanese: ‥‥動機は立証された。 次へ行くぜ。
English: The motive is clear. Let's move on.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Ugggh...

Japanese: あの晩、午前１時。 殺人現場で何があったか‥‥？
English: What happened at the crime scene at 1 in the morning, Mr. DeLite?

Japanese: さあ、話してみな！ ‥‥聞いてやるぜ。
English: Come now! Tell us! We're all ears.

Japanese: ～社長室で起こったこと～
English: -- At the CEO's Office --

Japanese: 社長室に入ったら、目の前に アヤシイ人影がヌッと出てきて‥‥
English: When I entered the office, there was a suspicious shadow there...

Japanese: いきなりヒタイを殴られたんです。 ボク、くらくらしちゃいました。
English: Suddenly, I was hit on the forehead. After that, I remember being a bit dazed.

Japanese: ホント、あれをつけてなかったら 死んでたところですよ！
English: If I hadn't been wearing that, I would have been killed!

Japanese: 気がついたら‥‥しゃ、社長の 死体が転がっていたんです！
English: When I came to... Mr. Bullard was lying there... dead!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ “いきなり殴られた”‥‥ですか。
English: I see... Suddenly hit on the forehead, huh?

Japanese: たしか、きのうの探偵も そんな証言をしていましたな。
English: I believe the detective from yesterday provided similar testimony.

Japanese: “怪人に後ろアタマを殴られた” ‥‥とか。
English: He said that Mask☆DeMasque struck him on the head from behind.

Japanese: （ケッキョク‥‥それはウソで、 哀牙自身が犯人だったけど）
English: (Of course, since Atmey turned out to be the culprit himself, that was all a lie.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
ヘタなウソをヘタにマネしても、 だれもダマせねえぜ。
English: No one's going to believe a pathetic lie like that.

Japanese: そ、そんな！ ボク、ホントにやられたんですよ！
English: Wh-What are you saying!? I really was attacked!

Japanese: あなたの証言がホントかどうか‥‥ 尋問で、アキラカになるでしょう。
English: We'll find out if what you say is true or not during the cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥いいな、まるほどう。 依頼人だからって、手を抜くなよ。
English: Got that, Mr. Trite? Don't go easy just because he's your client.

Japanese: そんなそぶりを見せたら‥‥ このコーヒー、おごっちゃうぜ！
English: If I see any sign that you are, I'll treat you to another cup of my special blend!

Japanese: ‥‥そのシンパイはいらないよ、 ゴドー検事。
English: You don't need to worry about that, Mr. Godot...

Japanese: （優作くんは、やっていない。 それを信じるんだ‥‥！）
English: (I have faith in Ron... I know he didn't do it...!)

Japanese: ～社長室で起こったこと～
English: -- At the CEO's Office --

Japanese: 社長室に入ったら、目の前に アヤシイ人影がヌッと出てきて‥‥
English: When I entered the office, there was a suspicious shadow there...

Japanese: いきなりヒタイを殴られたんです。 ボク、くらくらしちゃいました。
English: Suddenly, I was hit on the forehead. After that, I remember being a bit dazed.

Japanese: ホント、あれをつけてなかったら 死んでたところですよ！
English: If I hadn't been wearing that, I would have been killed!

Japanese: 気がついたら‥‥しゃ、社長の 死体が転がっていたんです！
English: When I came to... Mr. Bullard was lying there... dead!

Japanese: このへんの優作さんのハナシ‥‥ きのう、聞いたよね。
English: We heard this from Mr. DeLite yesterday, didn't we?

Japanese: 今回も‥‥少ないな。 優作くんの証言。
English: There's not much in this testimony either.

Japanese: やっぱり、何か かくしてるのかな。
English: I betcha he's still hiding something.

Japanese: 気の小さい優作くんのことだ。 とにかく、情報を引き出そうか。
English: I wouldn't be surprised. We'll just have to draw it out of him.

Japanese: ヘンなハナシが出て 来なきゃいいけどなあ‥‥。
English: I just hope he doesn't make things any more complicated...

Japanese: 仮面マスクの衣装を着て いなかったら、死んでましたよ！
English: I'd have been killed if I hadn't been wearing my Mask☆DeMasque costume!

Japanese: あわてて金庫に死体をかくして。 １０分ぐらいかかっちゃいました。
English: I panicked and hid the body in the safe. It took about 10 minutes.

Japanese: その“アヤシイ人影”‥‥ だれだったんですか？
English: Who was this "suspicious shadow"?

Japanese: いやー、いくらボクがアレでも、 わかっていれば言いますよ、名前。
English: If there were a thousand of me, and if even one knew, I'd tell you. Trust me.

Japanese: ‥‥ヘラヘラ笑ってるよ。
English: ...His dodging all of our questions is NOT helping us win his case.

Japanese: じゃあ、そのとき被害者の ブスジマ社長は、どうでしたか？
English: OK, then how was the victim, Mr. Bullard, at that time?

Japanese: “どう”と言いますと？
English: What do you mean by "how was he"?

Japanese: すでに殺害されていたのか？ まだ生きていたのか？
English: Was he already dead? Was he still alive?

Japanese: あるいは、殴りかかってきたのが ブスジマさん本人だった、とか。
English: Maybe he was the one who hit you in the first place.

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥。 どうなんでしょう？　成歩堂さん。
English: That's a good question... What do you think, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいです。
English: ...F-Forget it.

Japanese: ヒタイを‥‥？
English: Your forehead...?

Japanese: ええ。部屋の中に入った 瞬間に、ガツンと。
English: Yes. I was hit on the forehead as soon as I entered the room.

Japanese: そうとうなパワー、 そしてスピードでしたね。
English: It was an amazingly fast and powerful attack.

Japanese: 殴りかかってきた人物のコト、 何もおぼえてないんですか。
English: Do you remember anything about who hit you?

Japanese: まあ‥‥そうとうなパワー、 そしてスピードでしたからねえ。
English: Well, like I said... It was a fast and powerful hit.

Japanese: ちょっと、ボンヤリ しちゃったんだと思います。
English: So I think I was a little dazed for a while.

Japanese: ‥‥たよりにならないねえ、 優作さん。
English: I don't think Mr. DeLite even grasped what you were asking...

Japanese: ぼくも、ちょっと殴りつけて やりたくなってきたよ。
English: Yeah, I'd like to show him a fast and powerful attack myself.

Japanese: ‥‥そうとうなパワー、 そしてスピードで。
English: Maybe that would knock some sense back into him...

Japanese: “あれ”というのは‥‥？
English: "That"? Could you please clarify what you are referring to?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃないですか。 怪人☆仮面マスクの衣装ですよ！
English: Why, my Mask☆DeMasque costume, of course!

Japanese: まま、待ちなさい！ “かめんますく”‥‥？
English: W-Wait just a moment! "Mask☆DeMasque"...?

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。そういえばコレ、 言いませんでしたっけ。
English: Huh? Oh, did I forget to mention it before...?

Japanese: ボク、念のために 怪人のカッコウをしていたんです！
English: Just to be on the safe side, I dressed as Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: そして、ＫＢ警備の社長室に 華麗に舞いおりましたーーーーッ！
English: And then, I descended upon the office of the CEO of KB Security!

Japanese: なんですとおおおおッ！
English: Whaaaaaat!?

Japanese: な、なるほどくん、知ってた？ あたし、初めて聞いたよ。
English: Nick! Did you know about this? He never told me this!

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥ぼくも初耳だぞ）
English: (I don't recall him ever mentioning it to me either...)

Japanese: このオレですら、知らなかった。
English: Even I didn't know that.

Japanese: ‥‥つくづく、かくしゴトが 好きなボウヤだぜ。
English: It seems our little friend really loves to keep secrets.

Japanese: スミマセン。今まで、言い出す チャンスがなくて‥‥‥‥いや。
English: I'm sorry. I just never had a chance to mention it up until now... Wait.

Japanese: そんなコトないか。なんて言うか、 言いそびれちゃうコトってあるよな
English: That's not right. Umm... You know how sometimes things just slip your mind

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　６杯目のコーヒーが、 さめた視線でオレを見つめてるぜ。
English: Ha...! My 6th cup of coffee is staring up at me coldly.

Japanese: とにかく、今の証言は ムシできません。
English: At any rate, we can't ignore this new piece of information.

Japanese: 証人！　証言を修正するように。
English: Witness! Please correct your testimony.

Japanese: “気がついたら”‥‥？
English: "When you came to"...?

Japanese: ええ。部屋のすみっこで、 床の上にノビていたんです。
English: Yes. I was passed out in the corner of the room.

Japanese: ‥‥きみを殴ったヤツは、 いつの間に、どこへ消えたのかな。
English: Do you know when your assailant made their escape, and to where...?

Japanese: そ、それが‥‥よく おぼえてないんです。
English: I-I'm afraid... I don't really remember.
Huh...?

Japanese: アイツに殴られたとき、ワケが わからなくなっちゃって。
English: Once they hit me, I lost all track of what was going on.

Japanese: そのスキに逃げちゃったんだと 思いますけど‥‥。
English: I think that's when they made their escape...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ずいぶん ツゴウのいいハナシだぜ。
English: Ha...! That's certainly a convenient story for you, isn't it?

Japanese: 本当にまあ、ずいぶんとツゴウの いいハナシですな、弁護人。
English: It certainly is, isn't it, Mr. Wright? It almost sounds... practiced.

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくにヒニクを 言われても‥‥。
English: I-I don't know why you're telling me...

Japanese: また、どうして怪人のカッコウを？
English: Why were you dressed up as Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: いや、だってボク、 怪人☆仮面マスクですから。
English: Why... because I'm Mask☆DeMasque, of course.

Japanese: な、ナニを言い出すのですか！
English: W-What are you talking about!?

Japanese: 仮面マスクなら、おとなりの法廷で たった今、裁かれてる最中です！
English: Mask☆DeMasque's trial is being held next door!

Japanese: あ‥‥。そ、そうみたいですケド。 とにかく、あの時のボクは‥‥
English: Ah... Y-Yes, I guess so. Anyway, at that time...

Japanese: 怪人のコトで脅迫されたと 思っていたものですから。
English: ...I thought I was being blackmailed over the Mask☆DeMasque issue.

Japanese: やはりここは、怪人のカッコウで 行くべきだろう、と思ったんです。
English: So I thought I should go as him, just to be safe.
Oh boy...

Japanese: 衣装のマントがジャマで、 すごく動きづらくて‥‥
English: Let me tell you, it's a real pain to move around with that cape...

Japanese: 思ったより時間が かかっちゃって、タイヘンでした。
English: That's why it took a lot longer than I'd expected.

Japanese: “時間がかかった”‥‥？ （いったい、なんのことだ‥‥）
English: "Took a lot longer"...? (What is he talking about...?)

Japanese: （やっぱり、今は ようすを見よう‥‥）
English: (I think I'll wait and see how this develops...)

Japanese: ど、どうしてやめちゃうの？
English: Hey, why aren't you doing anything?

Japanese: たぶん、ヤッカイなコトに なるからね。
English: Because it'll probably end up causing more trouble.

Japanese: ‥‥ココロのジュンビが できてからにするよ。
English: I'll tackle it once I'm mentally prepared.

Japanese: うう‥‥じゃ、あたしも カクゴを決めとくね。
English: I guess I should prepare myself for the worst, too.

Japanese: あの‥‥“時間がかかった” というのは‥‥？
English: Um... What do you mean by "took a lot longer"...?

Japanese: あ。もちろん、アレですよ。
English: Oh, opening the safe, of course.

Japanese: 金庫のトビラに、マントが 引っかかっちゃったんです。
English: My cape got caught on the safe door, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥社長の死体をかくした ときのコトですけど‥‥
English: This all happened when I was hiding Mr. Bullard's body...

Japanese: なな‥‥なんですか！ ちょっと待ちなさいッ！
English: Wh-Wh-What was that!? Back up a second!

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: あなたが‥‥死体を！ 金庫にかくしたのですか！
English: You were the one that hid the body in the safe!?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥まあ。
English: Um, well... Yeah.

Japanese: なんですっとおおおおおおおおォ！
English: Inconceivablllllle!

Japanese: なぜ！　いったいどうして！ どんな理由で！　どういう意図で！
English: Why!? Just... Why!? What reason could you have!? What were you thinking!?

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトがパンツをセンタク機に ほうりこむのは、どんなときだ？
English: Question. When does someone toss their dirty shorts in the washing machine?

Japanese: はは‥‥はい？
English: Uh... What?

Japanese: コタエはひとつ。 ‥‥それを脱いだとき、だぜ。
English: The answer is simple. When they take them off.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥あいかわらず、 イミがわかりません。
English: As usual, I have no idea what you're saying.

Japanese: 『死体を金庫にかくしたのは、自分 が犯人だから』ってコトですか？
English: Do you mean that Mr. DeLite hid the body because he's the murderer?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ わかってるじゃねえか。
English: Ha...! So you're not as stupid as you look!

Japanese: 毎回、わかりにくいよ。 たとえ話‥‥
English: His metaphor this time was really obscure...

Japanese: 弁護人。もしかして‥‥
English: Mr. Wright, you don't mean...

Japanese: 今の、被告人のハナシ‥‥ あなたは知っていたのですか？
English: ...that you knew about this whole safe business, do you!?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥まあ。
English: Uh, well... Yes.

Japanese: なんですか！ 私だけ仲間はずれですかッ！
English: Why am I the only one not in the loop here!?

Japanese: 証人！　今の発言を、証言の シリにでも、つけ加えなさいッ！
English: Witness! Make sure you add this to the testimony!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥ロコツに キゲンが悪くなっちまったな）
English: (Uh oh... Looks like a storm front is moving in over the fair-weather judge.)

Japanese: どうして死体を金庫なんかに かくそうと思ったんですか？
English: Why did you hide the body in the safe anyway?

Japanese: だって‥‥ホラ。引き出しには 入らないじゃないですか、死体は。
English: Well... Because it wouldn't fit in a drawer.

Japanese: ‥‥イヤ。 そういうコトじゃなくて。
English: Uh, that's not exactly what I meant...

Japanese: ボク、死体を見て、ナニがなんだか わからなくなっちゃって。
English: When I saw that corpse, I kind of lost it.

Japanese: “死体が見つかったら疑われる！” ‥‥そう思ったんです。
English: I thought, "If they find his corpse, they'll think I did it!"

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　もっとシンプルな 理由だったんだろうぜ。
English: Ha...! I think you had a simpler reason than that.

Japanese: テメエが犯人だった！　だから、 １０分もかけて、死体をかくした。
English: It's because you killed him! That's why you spent 10 minutes hiding the body.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、 わかりやすい理由ですな。
English: Hmmm... That certainly makes more sense.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: （‥‥って、待てよ。 “１０分”‥‥だって？）
English: (Hold on... "10 minutes"...?)

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん！
English: What is it, Nick?

Japanese: ふと思ったんだけど‥‥
English: I just had a thought...

Japanese: あの晩‥‥あの状況で、ふつう 死体をかくそうとするかな‥‥？
English: Under those circumstances, would you normally try to hide the body...?

Japanese: しかも‥‥１０分もかけて。
English: And spend 10 whole minutes to do it?

Japanese: “あの状況”‥‥？ ね。なるほどくん！
English: Under "those circumstances"? What circumstances...? ...Oh!

Japanese: 優作さんが死体をかくすのが 不自然な状況だったのなら、
English: Hey, Nick! If you think his behavior was so strange...

Japanese: ‥‥その理由を示す 証拠をつきつけてみようよ！
English: ...why don't you present some evidence that would show just what those circumstances were!

Japanese: （“つきつける”‥‥そうか！ 法廷記録を見てみるんだ‥‥）
English: (That's it! I'll take a look at the Court Record, and present some evidence!)

Japanese: この記録を見てください。
English: Your Honor, could you please take a look at this record?

Japanese: それは‥‥なんですかな？
English: And what might this be?

Japanese: 社長室と警備室をつなぐ、 非常ブザーの記録です。
English: The record for the emergency buzzer that connects the CEO's office to security.

Japanese: 社長室のボタンを押せば、 警備員が飛んでくる‥‥。
English: If the button in the office is pressed, a security team is supposed to come running...

Japanese: 記録によると‥‥午前１時２分。 ブザーが１回、鳴っています！
English: And according to this record, the buzzer was pushed once, at 1:02 AM!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh... Whaaat?

Japanese: ‥‥もし、天杉　優作が 社長を殺害した犯人ならば！
English: If Mr. Ron DeLite truly was the murderer...

Japanese: 非常ブザーが鳴った瞬間、 すぐに逃げるはずなのです！
English: ...he would have ran as soon as that buzzer sounded!

Japanese: 警備員が、こちらに 向かっているわけですからね！
English: After all, a security guard would've been heading his way!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ トボけたボウヤのコトだ‥‥
English: Ha...! Let's remember who we're dealing with here.

Japanese: ビルに警備員がいることを 知らなかったんだろうぜ！
English: He probably had no idea there were security personnel in the building!

Japanese: 優作くんは１年前まで、 ＫＢ警備で隊長をつとめていた！
English: Up until one year ago, my client was working as a chief of security!

Japanese: 警備員のことを 知らないはずがありません！
English: There's no way he wouldn't have known about them!

Japanese: しかし！　ジッサイ、 警備員は来なかったんだぜ。
English: But! As it turns out, the guard never came.

Japanese: それは、あくまでもグーゼンです！
English: That was nothing more than a coincidence!

Japanese: 警備員が、どうしようもなく イイカゲンなダメ人間で、
English: The fact that the guard was a pathetic loser...

Japanese: コイビトの新しい彼氏に パンチを食らっていたことなど‥‥
English: ...who had just gotten punched in the face by his ex's new boyfriend...

Japanese: ‥‥被告人・優作くんが 知っていたはずがない！
English: ...and wasn't anywhere in the vicinity was not something Mr. DeLite could have known!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ トボけたボウヤのコトだ‥‥
English: Ha...! Again, remember who we're dealing with here.

Japanese: 非常ブザーが鳴らされたことに 気づかなかったんだろうぜ！
English: It's a sure bet that Mr. DeLite didn't even notice the buzzer going off!

Japanese: ブザーは、かなりスゴい音です。 聞き逃すことは、あり得ない！
English: This buzzer is extremely loud. There is no way he could've ignored something like that!

Japanese: ‥‥もし、優作くんに 意識があったならば‥‥ね。
English: IF he had been conscious, that is.

Japanese: “意識が”‥‥？ それは、どういうことですかッ！
English: "Conscious"...? What do you mean by that!?
Ugh...!

Japanese: アンタの話‥‥ 聞こうじゃねえか。
English: Fine. Let's hear your theory.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人の証言を 思い出してください。
English: Remember the defendant's testimony.

Japanese: 社長室に入った瞬間、 彼は何者かに殴られた‥‥。
English: The moment he entered the victim's office, someone attacked him...

Japanese: 本人は“くらくらした”と 言っていましたが‥‥
English: Mr. DeLite said he felt "dazed"...

Japanese: ジッサイは‥‥数分間、 意識を失っていたのでしょう。
English: I'm willing to wager that he was knocked unconscious for at least a few minutes.

Japanese: 意識を‥‥？　つまり、 キゼツしていたのですか！
English: Unconscious...? So he fainted!?

Japanese: 優作くんは、ブザーが 鳴ったのを知らなかった。
English: That's why Mr. DeLite didn't know that the buzzer had sounded.

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥ノンキに、死体を 金庫にかくしたのです！
English: And that's why... he thought he had time to hide the body!

Japanese: ‥‥そ‥‥それが なんだって言うんだ‥‥！
English: S-So what are you trying to say...!?

Japanese: 優作くんはキゼツさせられて、 そのあいだにブザーが鳴った‥‥
English: Mr. DeLite was knocked out, and the buzzer went off soon afterwards...

Japanese: そのジジツが物語るのは、 たったひとつ。
English: Now, unless my client was able to hit the buzzer while he was unconscious...

Japanese: あの部屋には、もう１人、 ベツの人間がいたのです！
English: It can only mean that there was another person in that room!

Japanese: そう！　天杉　優作を殴りつけ、 非常ブザーを鳴らした人物が！
English: That's right! Whoever it was knocked out Ron DeLite and then pressed the buzzer!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！ 弁護人！　それは、いったい‥‥
English: O-Order in the court! Mr. Wright! This... This is...!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！ このボウヤが‥‥
English: Th-This is preposterous! It was this kid...

Japanese: ‥‥天杉　優作が、 社長をやっつけたんだよ！
English: ...Ron DeLite, is the one who killed Kane Bullard!

Japanese: じゃあ、非常ブザーを 鳴らしたのは、だれですか？
English: Then who pressed the buzzer?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: I-It was...

Japanese: ひ、被害者しかいねえ。 ‥‥そうさ！
English: The victim, of course!

Japanese: ボウヤに襲われたとき、 社長が自分で押したんだろうぜ！
English: He pressed the buzzer when the defendant attacked him!

Japanese: 被害者は即死じゃなかった！ それぐらい、できたはずだぜ！
English: He didn't die right away! He must have held on long enough to push that button!
ACK!!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥毒島社長がみずから、 ブザーを鳴らした‥‥
English: Hmmm... So Kane Bullard sounded the buzzer himself...

Japanese: いかがですか？　成歩堂くん！
English: What is your opinion on this, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: （‥‥ここは、なんとしても 真犯人の存在を立証したい！）
English: (I need to prove that the real criminal was there at the scene! But how?)

Japanese: （ブザーを鳴らしたのは、社長じゃ なかった‥‥立証できるか？）
English: (Can I prove that it wasn't Kane Bullard who sounded the buzzer?)

Japanese: ‥‥証拠は‥‥ありません。
English: The defense... admits there is no evidence to the contrary.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥、被害者自身が ブザーを鳴らしたのかも‥‥
English: It is possible that the victim himself sounded the buzzer...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ たぶん、そうだったんだろうぜ。
English: Ha...! "Possible"? More like "definite", Mr. Trite.

Japanese: つまり‥‥やはり、現場には ふたりしかいなかったわけですな。
English: Which means... Only two people were there at the crime scene after all.

Japanese: 襲われた、ブスジマ社長と、 襲った犯人‥‥天杉　優作。
English: The victim, Kane Bullard, and his killer... Mr. DeLite.

Japanese: だ、ダメだよ、なるほどくん！
English: Come on, Nick!

Japanese: “真犯人がいた”って 立証できなかったら‥‥
English: If you can't prove that the "real killer was there"...

Japanese: 裁判、もう終わっちゃうよ！
English: ...this trial is as good as finished!

Japanese: （くそ！　やっぱり、 証拠を提示するしかない‥‥！）
English: (Argh! I guess I need to show some sort of evidence after all...!)

Japanese: わかりました。 決定的な証拠を提示します！
English: The defense's opinion is this, Your Honor!

Japanese: 非常ブザーを鳴らしたのが、 被害者ではなかった証拠は‥‥！
English: This piece of evidence proves that it wasn't the victim who sounded the buzzer!

Japanese: いかがですか？　ゴドー検事！
English: Well, what do you think, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 聞こえるぜ‥‥ブザーの音が！
English: Ha...! I can hear it... The sound of a buzzer!

Japanese: はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 今、アンタのアタマの中に 鳴りひびいているぜ。
English: It's going off inside your head at this very moment.

Japanese: ‥‥ココロの非常ブザーが！
English: ...The emergency buzzer of your heart!

Japanese: 鳴りひびいてるの？
English: Is it really?

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと。
English: ...Maybe just a little.

Japanese: 弁護人！　もっと しっかり考えなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! You need to think more carefully!

Japanese: ううう‥‥メイタンテーに なりそこねちゃったね。
English: Oof... Guess there goes your chance to become an "Ace Detective".

Japanese: （耳をかたむけるんだ！　美しき スイリがささやく真実に‥‥）
English: Come, listen more carefully! Maybe the truth will be elegantly revealed...?

Japanese: アイガ探偵になりきってるよ‥‥
English: Creepy... You sound like Detective Atmey...

Japanese: これが、決定的な証拠です！
English: I believe this is the piece of incontrovertible evidence you were looking for!

Japanese: ひ、非常ブザー‥‥ですか！
English: Th-The emergency buzzer...?

Japanese: いったい、どんな手がかりが 残ってるのですか？
English: Is there some kind of clue on it?

Japanese: ‥‥何も残っていません。
English: Absolutely not.

Japanese: おいおい‥‥開きなおる前に、 そのクチを閉じなおしたらどうだ？
English: Hey, come on now... At least give some thought to what you say before opening your mouth.

Japanese: “何も残っていない”‥‥ それこそが、手がかりなんですよ。
English: The fact that there are absolutely no clues... is itself the clue.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Now I'm the one who's clueless!

Japanese: このボタンには、 指紋が残っていません。
English: This button has no fingerprints on it.

Japanese: もし、ブスジマ社長が このボタンを押したのならば‥‥
English: If Mr. Bullard had really pressed it himself...

Japanese: 当然、指紋が残っているハズです！
English: ...naturally, he would have left his fingerprints behind!

Japanese: ぶふおおおおおおおお‥‥ッ！
English: Phwoooooooooooh!!

Japanese: ‥‥し‥‥指紋は‥‥ そこのボウヤがふきとったのさ！
English: R...Ron DeLite... obviously wiped them off!

Japanese: いったい、なぜ！　警備員が かけつけてくるかもしれないのに！
English: Why would he? A guard could have come in at any moment!

Japanese: 自分もさわっちまったんだよ！ そのボタンにな！
English: He touched that button! I know he did!

Japanese: 被告人‥‥優作くんは、 仮面マスクの衣装を着ていた。
English: The defendant, Mr. DeLite, was dressed as Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: 仮面マスクは‥‥手袋をしている！
English: And Mask☆DeMasque... always wears gloves!

Japanese: そもそも、指紋をふく必要なんて ないじゃないですかッ！
English: What reason could he possibly have had to wipe the button free of fingerprints!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら‥‥
English: Ha...! It would seem...

Japanese: イッパイ、食わされたようだぜ。
English: I've been forced to eat crow!

Japanese: イッパイ、飲んでるよ‥‥ コーヒー。
English: I wonder what blend number crow-flavored coffee is?

Japanese: しかし‥‥もし、現場に 真犯人がいたとして‥‥
English: However, if the real killer was there at the scene...

Japanese: どうして自ら、非常ブザーを 鳴らしたのでしょうか？
English: ...why would that person press the emergency buzzer?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなキケンをおかさず、 さっさと逃げるべきだったのでは？
English: Shouldn't they have run away without putting themselves in extra danger?

Japanese: なな、なんですか！ この、イヤな“間”は！
English: What's with this awkward silence all of a sudden!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら、 手品のタネも切れたようだぜ。
English: Ha...! It looks like you're fresh out of parlor tricks.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　バレてるよ！
English: They're on to you, Nick!

Japanese: と、とにかく 整理して考えてみよう‥‥！
English: J-Just give me a minute to collect my thoughts...!

Japanese: 真犯人は、午前１時に ブスジマ社長を殺害した。
English: The real culprit killed Mr. Bullard at around 1 AM.

Japanese: そのシュラバに、優作さんが ノコノコやって来たんだよね？
English: And Mr. DeLite just happened to waltz in when the murder was taking place, right?

Japanese: 犯人は優作くんを殴って、 そして‥‥非常ブザーを鳴らした。
English: The killer clobbered Mr. DeLite, and then sounded the buzzer.

Japanese: そんなコトしたら、すぐに 警備員が来ちゃうのにね。
English: Even though security was supposed to respond right away if the buzzer was pressed.

Japanese: （“警備員が”‥‥“来る”？）
English: ("Security was supposed to respond"? Hmm...)

Japanese: ‥‥時間切れです。
English: ...Time's up, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 弁護人。 あなたの考えを聞きましょう。
English: Let's hear what you have to say.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well then...

Japanese: ほお‥‥テキながら イイ目、してるじゃねえか。
English: Oh ho... You've got some guts. I like that in an opponent.

Japanese: “真犯人”は、なぜ 非常ブザーを押したのですか？
English: Why did the "real killer" sound the emergency buzzer?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥犯人は、 押すつもりはなかったのです。
English: In all probability... the killer didn't mean to press it.

Japanese: どういうことですか‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: まちがって押した‥‥ そう考えられます。
English: It's possible that they simply pressed it by accident.

Japanese: たとえば‥‥格闘中、 背中で押してしまった、とか。
English: It could have been with their back during the scuffle, for example.

Japanese: あ。だから指紋が 残っていなかったんですね！
English: Aha! That must be why there weren't any fingerprints!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そのギザギザアタマ ‥‥つめものは入ってるのかい？
English: Ha...! Is there a functioning brain underneath all that ridiculous hair, Mr. Trite?

Japanese: はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ボウヤの証言じゃ、殴られたのは 部屋に入った瞬間だった。
English: The accused testified that he was hit as soon as he entered the room.

Japanese: ブザーのボタンから、イチバン 遠い場所じゃねえか！
English: In other words, as far away from the buzzer as he could possibly be at the time!

Japanese: ‥‥あぐッ！
English: ...Ack!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥、してやられた みたいですな、弁護人。
English: It would seem you've been taken for a ride, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （くそっ‥‥もう一度！ あわてず急いで考えるんだ！）
English: (Argh! One more time! I've got to calm down and think quick!)

Japanese: おそらく‥‥犯人は、あれが なんのボタンか、知らなかった。
English: In all probability... the killer didn't know what the button was for.

Japanese: どういうことですか‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: 『これ、なんのボタンだろう？』 そう思って、押してみたのです！
English: The killer probably thought, "I wonder what this does?" and pressed it to find out!

Japanese: あ。ボクもよく、 それでシッパイするんですよ！
English: Oh! That's exactly how I make a lot of my mistakes, too!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そのギザギザアタマ ‥‥つめものは入ってるのかい？
English: Ha...! Is there a functioning brain underneath all that ridiculous hair, Mr. Trite?

Japanese: はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ボタンのパネルには、ハッキリ 書いてあったはずだぜ。
English: The panel around the button is labeled quite clearly.

Japanese: 《非常ブザー》って、な。
English: It says, "Emergency Buzzer".

Japanese: ‥‥あぐッ！
English: Doh!

Japanese: それともナニか？
English: Let me guess.

Japanese: アンタと同じで、字が読めない ヤツだったのか？　犯人は！
English: Maybe next, you'll want to claim this mysterious "killer" was illiterate?

Japanese: （くそっ‥‥もう一度！ あわてず急いで考えるんだ！）
English: (Argh! One more time! I've got to calm down and think quick!)

Japanese: ボタンを押せば‥‥ 警備員が駆けつけてくる。
English: The killer knew that if they pressed that button, a guard would come running.

Japanese: 犯人の目的は、 まさにそれだったのです。
English: And that was exactly what they wanted.

Japanese: じ‥‥自分で呼んだ、 というのですか？　警備員を‥‥！
English: D-Do you mean to say the killer called the guard on purpose!?

Japanese: まあ‥‥。結果的には 来なかったんですけど。
English: Yes... Although as it turned out, he never showed up.

Japanese: パンチ食らってたからね。 ‥‥警備員さん。
English: Because he was getting his clock cleaned at the time...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 泣かせる犯人じゃねえか。
English: Ha...! What a touching story!

Japanese: 自分のツミを悔いあらためて、 自首しようとした、ってワケか？
English: You're saying the killer had a change of heart and called the guard to turn himself in?

Japanese: そうじゃありません。
English: No, I'm not.

Japanese: 非常ブザーが鳴ったとき、 社長室には３人の人物がいた。
English: When that buzzer sounded, there were three people in that office.

Japanese: 被害者の、ブスジマ社長。 彼は、すでに死んでいます。
English: The victim, Kane Bullard, who was already dead.

Japanese: 被告人の、優作くん。 彼は、殴られてキゼツしています。
English: The defendant, Ron DeLite, who was out cold.

Japanese: そして‥‥３人目が、 問題の“真犯人”です。
English: And... The third person. The real killer.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなりますね。
English: ...Hypothetically, yes.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Now then...

Japanese: この状況で、真犯人だけが 逃げたら‥‥どうなるでしょう？
English: In this situation, if the real killer made an escape, what would happen?

Japanese: 部屋の中に残るのは、 被害者と天杉　優作の、ふたり‥‥
English: The only ones left in the room would be the victim, and Ron DeLite...

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなトコに 警備員がやって来たら‥‥
English: And... And if any security guards came running in at that time...

Japanese: ダレが見たって、 ボクが犯人じゃないですかッ！
English: They would think that I was the murderer!

Japanese: そう！　それこそが 真犯人の目的だったのです。
English: Yes! That was precisely the real killer's objective.

Japanese: 天杉　優作に、 殺人のツミを着せることこそが！
English: To frame Ron DeLite for the murder!

Japanese: ぶぶぐほほおおおおおォッ‥‥ッ！
English: Phwooooooooooh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら‥‥
English: Ha...! It would seem...

Japanese: また、煮え湯を 飲まされたようだぜ。
English: I've been made to eat my words once again!

Japanese: また、飲んでるよ。 ‥‥煮えコーヒー。
English: Actually you've been made to do a spit-take... with a cup of coffee.

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん！ いったい、ダレなんですか！
English: M-Mr. Wright! Who was it!?

Japanese: ボクを犯人にしようとしたのは！ ‥‥‥‥いや。
English: Who was it that tried to frame me!? Err... Wait.

Japanese: 待てよ。ハンニンって言えば、 ボクもいちおう怪人だし。エラそう
English: Wait a second... I'm the one and only Mask☆DeMasque, so

Japanese: なるほどくん！　犯人って‥‥！
English: Nick! You mean the real killer is...!?

Japanese: これから、そいつを ここへ引きずり出してやるんだよ！
English: We're going to drag that person in here right now!

Japanese: で、でも！ いったい、だれなの？
English: But, but! Who is it?

Japanese: ハッキリした証拠はないけど ‥‥考えてみるしかない。
English: I don't have any solid proof yet... but think about it.

Japanese: 犯人は、優作くんの正体を 知っていた。そして、あの晩‥‥
English: The killer knew Mr. DeLite's identity. And they also knew...

Japanese: ＫＢ警備に呼び出されて いたことも知っていた‥‥。
English: ...that he had been called to KB Security that night...

Japanese: ヤツは、それを利用して‥‥ この殺人の計画を立てたのさ！
English: So, the killer used him to execute a well-crafted plan to murder Kane Bullard!

Japanese: それでは、うかがいます。
English: Hmm, now then. Let's hear your accusation, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 天杉　優作にツミを着せようとした 殺人犯とは‥‥だれなのですか！
English: Who was it that framed Mr. Ron DeLite for the murder of Kane Bullard!?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってよ、 なるほどくん！
English: H-Hang on, Nick!

Japanese: そのヒト、優作さんに ツミを着せようとしたの？
English: Are you saying this is the person who tried to frame Mr. DeLite for the murder?

Japanese: そうなんじゃないかな。
English: Well, yeah. Who else could it be?

Japanese: それにそのヒト、優作さんが 仮面マスクだって知ってたの？
English: Did they even know that Mr. DeLite was Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: ‥‥どうかなあ‥‥
English: Hmm, I wonder...

Japanese: コラ！　たたきつけた証拠を ほったらかさないように！
English: Mr. Wright! Need I warn you about what will happen if you present irrelevant evidence?

Japanese: （もう一度、考えなおした ほうがいいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I'd better think this through again...)

Japanese: もちろん‥‥犯人は、 奥さんのまれかさんです！
English: Naturally... the true culprit was none other than... Ron DeLite's wife, Desirée!

Japanese: な、なんですって！ いったい、それは‥‥
English: Wh-Whaaaat!? Why that's simply--

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん！ あ、あなたは‥‥なんという‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Wright!! Have you gone insane...!?

Japanese: ‥‥ひとつ‥‥忘れてないか？ まるほどう。
English: Aren't you forgetting something, Mr. Trite?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: E-Excuse me?

Japanese: あの晩、午前１時‥‥ 天杉　希華がどこにいたか‥‥？
English: Where was Desirée DeLite at 1:00 AM that night...?

Japanese: そうだよ！　まれかさんは‥‥
English: Ah! That's right!

Japanese: 警察署にいたんじゃない！ あの晩は、ずっと！
English: She was at the police station! For almost the entire night!

Japanese: バイクのスピード違反で、 取り調べを受けていた‥‥
English: She was being questioned after being stopped for reckless riding...

Japanese: ‥‥ここに、記録もあるぜ。
English: ...I have a record of it here.

Japanese: しまったァァッ！ （‥‥すっかり、忘れてた‥‥）
English: Aaaah!! (I forgot all about that...)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ボケ始めている ようですね、弁護人は。
English: It seems that senility may be setting in early for you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （うう‥‥裁判長に 言われちまった‥‥）
English: (Talk about things you never want to hear this judge say to you...)

Japanese: ‥‥他の可能性を 考えたほうがいいみたいだね。
English: It looks like you'd better think again.

Japanese: 名探偵・星威岳　哀牙‥‥ 彼しか考えられません。
English: Detective Luke Atmey... He's the only one who could have done it.

Japanese: 名探偵‥‥
English: Ace Detective...

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙‥‥
English: Luke Atmey...

Japanese: あの‥‥怪人☆仮面マスク、 ですか‥‥？
English: You mean... Mask☆DeMasque did it...?

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。たった今、となりの 法廷で裁かれているのは‥‥
English: Your Honor, the person being tried in the court next to us...

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクなんかでは ないのです。
English: ...is not Mask☆DeMasque at all.

Japanese: 彼こそが！　毒島　黒兵衛を 殺害した真犯人だったのです！
English: He is, in actuality... the true murderer of Kane Bullard!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ 弁護人！　せ、説明を‥‥
English: Order! Order!! M-Mr. Wright! Explain yourself!

Japanese: “窃盗罪”と“殺人罪”‥‥ 罪が重いのは、どちらでしょうか？
English: Theft and murder... Which is the more serious crime?

Japanese: それは‥‥くらべるまでもなく、 “殺人罪”でしょう。
English: They're not even close. Murder is the more serious crime, of course!

Japanese: 殺人罪は、現行法では 極刑の可能性もありますからな。
English: It's a capital crime subject to a capital punishment.

Japanese: ‥‥きのうの法廷を 思い出してください。
English: Please remember the trial from yesterday, if you would.

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙が自白したとき‥‥ 法廷は、大喝采のウズでした。
English: When Luke Atmey confessed, there was a huge commotion in the courtroom.

Japanese: 名探偵が大盗賊‥‥ 盛り上がるぜ、そいつは。
English: Of course. A famous detective was unmasked as... well, as Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: “殺人者”となるより、 “大盗賊”として有罪になる。
English: Instead of being convicted of murder, he was found guilty of grand larceny.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、星威岳　哀牙の 本当の狙いだったのです。
English: ...That was his true objective all along.

Japanese: “有罪”に‥‥
English: To be found "guilty"...

Japanese: 仮面マスクには、殺人が起こった 時刻のカンペキなアリバイがある。
English: Mask☆DeMasque had the perfect alibi for when the murder took place.

Japanese: このとおり‥‥高菱屋でツボを 盗んでいた、というアリバイが。
English: He was stealing the urn at Lordly Tailor.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: ...In other words!

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクとしての 《有罪判決》こそが‥‥
English: Being found guilty as Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙の アリバイ工作だったのです！
English: ...was Luke Atmey's airtight, watertight, and unassailable alibi.

Japanese: 有罪判決が‥‥ アリバイですってェェェェェッ！
English: A guilty verdict... AS AN ALIBI!?

Japanese: もう‥‥ そろそろ、時間だぜ。
English: You know... It's almost time.

Japanese: な、何がですか？
English: F-For what?

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙の判決、さ。 ムズカシイ裁判じゃねえからな。
English: For Luke Atmey's verdict. It was a pretty simple trial, after all.

Japanese: 休廷して判決を食い止めるなら ‥‥今しかねえぜ。
English: If we're going to stop this trial and stall that one, we need to do it now.

Japanese: とは言っても‥‥
English: Of course...

Japanese: 名探偵の殺人罪を立証できる 自信があれば、のハナシだが。
English: That's assuming you have proof that the detective was the one who committed the murder.

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙の裁判は、 日本中の注目を集めています。
English: Mr. Luke Atmey's trial has indeed attracted the attention of the entire country.

Japanese: それを中断させた上、もし 殺人罪の立証に失敗すれば‥‥
English: If we were to intrude, and failed to provide adequate proof of his true crime...

Japanese: 被告人・天杉　優作に対する 判決は、揺るぎないものになる！
English: Mr. DeLite would be left with no grounds for appeal.

Japanese: （いいのか、本当に‥‥）
English: (Am I really sure about this...?)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
ギャンブルで賭けていいのは‥‥ 自分のイノチ、までだぜ‥‥。
English: A bet's only good when your life's the ante.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　ボク‥‥ ボク、あなたを信じています！
English: Mr. Wright! I... I believe in you!

Japanese: ゆ、優作くん‥‥
English: Mr. DeLite...

Japanese: だから‥‥だから。 やっちゃってくださーーーい！
English: So... So... Please. I'M BEGGING YOOOOOOU!!

Japanese: （そ、そうは言っても‥‥）
English: (Thanks... But...)

Japanese: （ぼくの決断で‥‥ 優作くんの人生が、決まる！）
English: (My decision will determine the rest of your life!)

Japanese: （ぼくに‥‥そんなことが ユルされるのか‥‥？）
English: (Can I really risk your life like this...?)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: ...Phoenix...

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ （なんだ、今のは‥‥）
English: (Wh-What was that...?)

Japanese: ‥‥迷ってはダメ。 なるほどくん‥‥
English: ...Don't stray Phoenix...

Japanese: ‥‥依頼人のために、 信じた道を進むの‥‥
English: ...For your client... Take the path of trust...

Japanese: （こ‥‥この声は‥‥ ‥‥この声は‥‥‥‥）
English: (Th-That voice... It-It sounds like...)

Japanese: （‥‥ちひろ‥‥さん！）
裁判長！　ただちに 休廷を要請します！
English: Your Honor! The defense requests an immediate recess!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ それが‥‥アンタのコタエかい。
English: Ha...! So that's your answer, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。 私もハラを決めました。
English: Very well. I've decided as well.

Japanese: これより２０分間、 本法廷は中断します。
English: This court will now take a 20 minute recess.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。 星威岳　哀牙を召喚しなさい！
English: ...Mr. Wright. When we return, please summon Mr. Luke Atmey to the stand!

Japanese: はいっ！
English: Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時５８分 地方裁判所　第５法廷
English: October 14, 11:58 AM District Court Courtroom No. 5

Japanese: うふふふふふふ‥‥ いかがですかな？　アイガ探偵殿！
English: Hee hee hee hee... Well, Sir Detective Atmey!?

Japanese: フッ‥‥‥フッフッフッ‥‥
English: Heh... Heh heh heh...

Japanese: あいや、アウチ検事殿。 ‥‥なかなか、やりますな。
English: I have to say, Mr. Payne. You performed splendidly.

Japanese: あいや、アイガ探偵殿。 アナタのほうこそ‥‥
English: Oh no, Sir Detective Atmey. You were the one who...

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: 被告人、星威岳　哀牙に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Luke Atmey...

Japanese: ‥‥その判決‥‥
English: Wait!

Japanese: ちょっと、待っていただきたいッ！
English: Don't hand down your verdict yet! Please!

Japanese: ‥‥これはこれは‥‥弁護士殿。 我が法廷へ、ようこそ‥‥。
English: Well well... Sir Lawyer. Welcome to my courtroom...

Japanese: なんですか、あなたは！
English: Who's this hoser, eh!?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくは、弁護士の 成歩堂　龍一です。
English: My name is Phoenix Wright, attorney at law.

Japanese: たった今‥‥その男‥‥ 星威岳　哀牙を告発します。
English: And I wish to file an accusation against this man, Luke Atmey!

Japanese: こ、告発‥‥？　この、 怪人☆仮面マスクを、ですかな？
English: Ac-Accusation? You accuse Mask☆DeMasque--

Japanese: ‥‥その人物は 怪人なんかじゃない。
English: That man is not Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: ‥‥ただの殺人犯人なんですよ！
English: He's just a ruthless murderer!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですとォォォォォッ！
English: WH-WHAAAAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、天杉　優作に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ron DeLite...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
